sections.paragraph:
fut. יִגַּח to push, strike with the horn, used of horned animals, Exo 21:28, 31 Exo 21:31, 32 Exo 21:32. (This root is onomatopoetic. The idea of striking, or pushing, lies both in the syllable נג, נכ, compare נָגַע, נָגַף, נָגַן, נָגַל; and also in the other which ends in ח compare נָבַח to bark, properly to push, strike, as כָּלִב, נָדַח and نطح to push with the horn.)

Piel id., Eze 34:21 Dan 8:4 used figuratively of a victor, who prostrates the nations before him, Deu 33:17 1Ki 22:11 Psa 44:6. Hence-

Hithpael, to wage war with any one, Dan 11:40. Compare Chald. אַגִּיחַ קְרָבָא; followed by עִם to wage war with any one; in Ithpael id. Hence-
